content:
# Experiment

**Summary**: Laboratory testing to observe physical or chemical changes under controlled conditions.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-kassim|KASSIM, 2012]] used experiment to measure adsorption density of ions on limestone surfaces at 25°C and 90°C.

- [[2020-gowida|Gowida, 2020]] used experimental measurement to evaluate the performance of LTO and thermochemical heat generation on sand packs.

- [[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023]] conducted flow experiments in a drag reduction unit to measure pressure drop against flow rates.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2020-gowida|Gowida, 2020]] used experimental measurement to evaluate the performance of LTO and thermochemical heat generation on sand packs.

- [[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023]] conducted flow experiments in a drag reduction unit to measure pressure drop against flow rates.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Determined that calcium ion concentration is a primary driver for sulfate and chloride adsorption on limestone. ([[2012-kassim|KASSIM, 2012, p. 113]])

- The study successfully demonstrated that thermochemicals could raise temperatures from 80 to 188 °C in situ. ([[2020-gowida|Gowida, 2020, p. 17]])

- The PEAS showed lower drag compared to surfactant-based EAS at all tested temperatures (25, 40, 50 °C). ([[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023, p. 198]])
